research.status_summary: Walnut is operational and actively maintaining its platform with new AI features, but has been in a prolonged post-layoff stabilization phase since 2023–2024, with headcount roughly halved from ~120 to ~60–92 employees and no new funding since its January 2022 Series B.
research.hiring_policy: Walnut's jobs page is active (last updated Jan 2026) and ZoomInfo notes at least one open role (Assistant Controller, Israel); no public evidence of visa sponsorship or explicit EOR-friendly policy, and Wellfound lists offices only in New York City and Tel Aviv-Yafo, suggesting limited remote-first posture.
recent_news: AI push: Walnut repositioned as an 'AI-powered interactive demo platform' with new features including AI Mode (generate a full demo from a single prompt), StoryCaptureAI, and Smart Suggestions rolled out in 2025 and teased for further expansion in 2026.

Product expansion: Platform evolved beyond demo creation into a full revenue/GTM layer — adding Salesforce integration, PLG Journeys, Onboarding Flows, and Customer Success Hubs (2025 Wrapped blog, Jan 2026).

Headcount contraction history: A May 2024 Calcalist report noted a 20% workforce cut (≈15 people), the second round of layoffs since 2023, reducing total staff by ~50% from peak of 120 to ~60; as of April 2026 LeadIQ estimates ~92 employees, suggesting modest recovery.
research.growth_signal: No new funding since the $35M Series B in January 2022 (total ~$56M raised); headcount appears to have partially recovered to ~92 (LeadIQ, April 2026) from a post-layoff low, and product cadence is active with AI feature releases, but the company remains at Series B with no public signals of a new round.
research.fit_for_lior: Weak fit — Walnut is a sales-demo SaaS tool, not a Creative AI or generative product, and its current lean headcount (~92 employees), Israeli HQ, post-layoff cost discipline, and no fresh funding round make it a low-priority prospect for a Creative AI / Product Analyst / Vibe Coder profile.
